comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Plan-review PASS (advisory=6, signed). Triage: (1) T8 'respects_artifact_altitude question text undefined in plan' — valid; the design source (DSO gap report FP-1) supplies the exact semantics: 'does the finding demand detail or presume a choice the artifact, at its level, legitimately defers to a child/implementation?' — implementing with that text. (2) G1G2 'test_review_kernel_rules.py:77 hardcodes field count + stale 7-graded-sub-questions comment' — will update the comment (assertion stays valid as both sides grow by 2). (3) G1G2 test-placement — kernel-math tests (contract + validity comparability) go in test_review_kernel_rules.py; the committed_claim discrimination CASES go in plan-review-verifier.eval.yaml with a presence/polarity assertion in test_plan_review_evals.py (honors the AC intent + the reviewer's placement concern). (4) ADR advisories (E6/E1/E3) — creating the ADR at a checkable path (ls-able). No ticket edit (would stale the attestation).

compiled_state.description:
## Why
The generic Pass-2 verify contract applies to EVERY finding. Two new binary sub-answers close whole FP/defect classes; one gloss fixes a false-premise gap. Designed + calibrated together.

## What (verified code targets)
- The binary field set is DERIVED from decide.GRADED_BINARY by a uniform loop in verify.py (verification_model(), ~line 120-150), with per-field descriptions in _BINARY_DESC. So: (a) add committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim and respects_artifact_altitude to GRADED_BINARY in review_kernel/decide.py; (b) add their description strings to _BINARY_DESC in review_kernel/verify.py; (c) add their question text to plan_review_verifier.md + _agentic.md.
- na-default RECONCILIATION: because the field set is derived uniformly, "old sidecars stay comparable" means decide.validity() must treat an ABSENT/na answer as non-answerable (excluded from the graded fraction), not as 0. Confirm/adjust validity() so adding 2 keys does not retroactively penalize prior findings. This is the concrete meaning of "na-default".
- committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim (unifies G-4/G-7): "Does a committed element (AC / decomposed task / concrete edit / scope exclusion) depend on this claim, while the plan provides NEITHER verification NOR a defined behavior if it is false?" respects_artifact_altitude (FP-1): graded VALIDITY input (low score DROPS the FP), not a veto. FP-2 gloss on evidence_entails_finding: a finding demanding what the plan already states is not entailed. No new key.

## Provenance
Gap-report labels (G-*/FP-*/R-*/WS-N) are tags from ~/dso_rebar_plan_review_gap_report.md + session log brief-bride-rash (95f1-1b3b-a867-4020), NOT codebase symbols. Symbols introduced by a SIBLING story (e.g. committed_work_relies_on_unbacked_claim from WS1) are target-state, not pre-existing; the dependency is recorded.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Both keys added to decide.GRADED_BINARY + _BINARY_DESC + both verifier prompts. Proof: author `test_verifier_new_subanswers` in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py asserting the two fields appear in the verification contract; `pytest -k verifier_new_subanswers` green.
- [ ] validity() treats absent/na for the new keys as non-answerable (old-sidecar comparability). Proof: a unit test replays a pre-change finding and asserts identical validity.
- [ ] evidence_entails_finding restatement gloss in both verifier prompts. Proof: `grep -l "already states" src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_verifier*.md` returns both (target state).
- [ ] Rationale for graded-field-vs-prompt-gloss recorded in a new ADR under docs/adr/ (named location, not "somewhere").
- [ ] Calibration: RECALL case (well-formed ## Assumptions block with a fallback drops as low-validity) AND a PRECISION case (a genuinely-unbacked committed claim survives). Proof: author both in tests/unit/test_plan_review_evals.py; `pytest -k committed_claim` green.
- [ ] No criterion-specific branching added to Pass 2/3 (the two keys are generic GRADED_BINARY entries).

file_impact.reason: GRADED_BINARY tuple + SeverityAttrs: add the two new binary sub-answers here (shared kernel, not plan_review-local)
validity() iterates GRADED_BINARY; new sub-answers must be added there to participate in validity
verifier prompt: add the two sub-answer questions + evidence_entails_finding restatement gloss

compiled_state.title: WS1: Pass-2 vocabulary expansion (2 new generic sub-answers + 1 gloss)
